description:
You are an embedded software engineer at a medical robotics company. Fix three implementation bugs in the ArmController library within the Visual Studio 2022 solution. The reported failure modes are: PID controller oscillation and divergence, a joint limiter failing to prevent movement past hard stops, and incorrect velocity command scaling. Ensure the project builds with zero errors and all safety-critical issues are resolved.

Windows user: Docker, password: GymAnything123!

bugs: PID derivative term uses (error - previousError) instead of (previousError - error), causing positive feedback
JointLimiter clamps with inverted condition: returns value only when OUTSIDE limits instead of inside
VelocityScaler multiplies by 1000 (rad/s to mrad/s) when it should divide (mrad/s to rad/s)
